question_title: Will the community prediction be higher than 12.00% on 2026-03-14 for the Metaculus question "Will the North Atlantic sea surface temperature reach a daily record high before May 2026?"?
summary: This meta-prediction asks whether the Metaculus CP for the North Atlantic SST record question will be strictly above 12% on March 14. The CP has been on a clear, persistent downward trajectory from ~40% (Feb 9) to 12% (Mar 2), with no sustained reversals. The CP is currently oscillating between 11-12%, right at the threshold. Underlying fundamentals support continued decline: 2026 SSTs run below 2025, La Niña is weakening but not causing N. Atlantic warming, and each day without a record makes the event less likely. The strong downward trend and bearish fundamentals make it more likely the CP will be at or below 12% on March 14, but the proximity to the threshold creates some possibility of YES through small fluctuations.

factors.description: Strong persistent downward CP trend: 40% → 12% over ~3 weeks, with no reversals lasting more than a day or two
CP currently at or slightly below threshold (oscillating 11-12%), requiring strict >12.00% for YES
Underlying fundamentals: 2026 North Atlantic SSTs running below 2025, making a daily record unlikely and supporting continued CP decline
Time passing without a record: each day reduces the underlying probability further, pushing CP lower
Possibility of temporary upward bounce: 97 forecasters means moderate volatility, a few updates could push CP briefly above 12%
La Niña dissolving / potential El Niño transition could be interpreted as warming catalyst by some forecasters

reasoning:
**Forecast: 25% probability (YES)**

**Key reasoning:**

- **CP trajectory is strongly bearish**: The community prediction has fallen from ~40% (Feb 9) to 12% (Mar 2) in a persistent downward trend with no sustained reversals. This is the dominant signal.

- **CP is at the threshold**: The CP is currently oscillating between 11-12%, and the question requires *strictly* above 12.00%. This means the CP is already at or slightly below the threshold, not above it.

- **Fundamentals support continued decline**: 2026 North Atlantic SSTs are running below 2025 levels (per question background and WMO seasonal update). La Niña is dissolving but there's no indication of sudden North Atlantic warming. Each day without a record makes the underlying event less likely.

- **Why not lower?**: The 12-day window until resolution creates some uncertainty. With 97 forecasters, small updates could temporarily push the CP above 12%. A single forecaster revising upward could nudge it past the threshold. But the trend makes this increasingly unlikely as more time passes.
sources_consulted: [(Metaculus) Will the North Atlantic sea surface temperature reach a daily record high before May 2026?](https://www.metaculus.com/questions/41595)
